rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use visible identifiers from the current target component or implementation for AGREE expressions
Place assumptions in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by respecting the authoritative source of component names, ports, and connections
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs for output dependency contracts in component type annexes
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not delete generated clauses without diagnostic context unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
